mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing investors to sell equities and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in >60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which drives corporate revenue acceleration and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60d returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production reflects immediate capacity utilization and output growth, creating short-term positive momentum that translates to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no directional bias or negative correlation with INDPRO growth during early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Ultra-low policy rates combined with consistent monthly nonfarm payroll growth create a liquidity and earnings tailwind that pushes equity valuations higher, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of low rates and rising payrolls fails to generate positive 20d returns in >55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year decline in housing starts exceeding 10 percent signals a sharp contraction in residential construction activity, dragging down related equity sectors and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral in >65% of months where housing starts fall by more than 10 percent annually.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in the consumer price index trigger expectations of tighter monetary policy, which raises discount rates and compresses equity multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rising CPIAUCSL consistently coincides with positive 60-day equity returns, breaking the inverse correlation.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in the unemployment rate and increase in total nonfarm payrolls confirms robust labor market tightening, which supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The dual labor improvement signal fails to predict positive 20d returns with a rank correlation above 0.05 in mid-cycle samples.
